records.ety: [L. inquisitio : cf. F. inquisition. See Inquire, and cf. Inquest.]

defs: The act of inquiring; inquiry; search; examination; inspection; investigation.
quotes: As I could learn through earnest inquisition.
Let not search and inquisition quail To bring again these foolish runaways.

defs: The finding of a jury, especially such a finding under a writ of inquiry.
quotes: The justices in eyre had it formerly in charge to make inquisition concerning them by a jury of the county.

defs: A court or tribunal for the examination and punishment of heretics, fully established by Pope Gregory IX. in 1235. Its operations were chiefly confined to Spain, Portugal, and their dependencies, and a part of Italy.
To make inquisition concerning; to inquire into.
